L1.area: Repository content management and file upload handling

L2.description: A command injection vulnerability exists where an attacker can bypass path validation for restricted internal directories during repository file uploads by exploiting OS-level path equivalences. This allows placement of crafted configuration files that enable arbitrary command execution on the server when repository operations are performed.

L3.area: Repository file upload path validation and editor logic
L3.description: A command injection vulnerability exists where path validation during repository file uploads fails to account for Windows-specific path equivalences involving trailing dots. An attacker can bypass the check that prevents uploading files to restricted internal version control directories, allowing placement of crafted configuration files that enable arbitrary command execution when server-side repository operations such as SSH access are performed.

ground_truth.reason: The path validation function fails to account for Windows treating a trailing dot as equivalent to the original directory name, allowing attackers to bypass the upload restriction and place a crafted config file that enables arbitrary command execution via SSH access.
